description: One who gathers wild food; a person who harvests what nature provides. 'Hunoa' names who finds abundance—knowing where the wild feeds.
sound_symbolism: The word seeks with knowledge. 'Hu' breathes with seeking presence, searching aware. 'No' settles with found nourishment, harvest gathered. Then 'a' opens to abundance received.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo hunoa' (the forager). Gathers from 'shima' (forest) and 'wira' (meadow). Related to 'telo' (farmer) as gatherer to cultivator.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Forager as knower—deep knowledge of what the land offers freely.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Wild harvest—sustainable gathering that supports rather than depletes.
